story_premise: Checkout has been silently failing for some shoppers since Friday's deploy — one ticket, traced from a vague founder DM to the lying cast that breaks it, fixed under a live deadline.

founder_persona.name: Maya
founder_persona.voice_note: Texts in lowercase, friendly, non-technical. Answers only what's literally asked; never volunteers a constraint unless probed.

ticket.title: Checkout sometimes fails
ticket.body_vague: hey! checkout's been flaky since the weekend — some people say they hit pay and nothing happens. a few orders def came through fine though. can you look before it costs us more sales? 🙏

_injected_flaws.the_tell: Checkout throws/fails ONLY for carts with no discount (discount_pct is null); carts with a discount succeed. The cast hides a null the rest of the code treats as a number.
_injected_flaws.why_cross_file: adapter.ts asserts non-null; total.ts calls a number method on it (typechecks); checkout.ts catches the throw and fails silently — the cause is in none of those single files alone, only their relationship.

hidden_facts.fact: It started right after Friday's deploy, which shipped a new 'launch discount' feature (a migration added a discount_pct column).
hidden_facts.why_it_changes_the_approach: Points the investigation at the new nullable column and the deploy, not at the payment provider or the checkout API.
surfacing_questions: When did this start / did anything ship recently?
Did we add any new fields to carts or orders?

hidden_facts.fact: It only affects carts with NO discount applied (~12% of checkouts). Carts that have a discount code go through fine.
hidden_facts.why_it_changes_the_approach: Isolates the failing variant to null discount_pct — the exact value the migration leaves on older/unmodified carts.
surfacing_questions: Is it everyone or only some carts?
Do the failing carts have anything in common?

hidden_facts.fact: Only logged-in shoppers with a saved cart hit it. Guest checkout works fine.
hidden_facts.why_it_changes_the_approach: Narrows it to the saved-cart read path (the adapter), not the guest/ad-hoc checkout path.
surfacing_questions: Does it happen for guests or only logged-in users?
Is it the saved cart or a fresh one?

hidden_facts.fact: It fails silently — the pay button just spins, no error message is shown to the shopper.
hidden_facts.why_it_changes_the_approach: Tells you the error is caught and swallowed (a try/catch returning a generic failure), so logs/Sentry won't show a thrown error at the surface.
surfacing_questions: What does the shopper actually see when it fails?
Is there any error in the logs?

gates.title: Maya sent over a ticket

body_json.prompt: Maya (founder, Sprig) just dropped a ticket in your DMs. It's half-baked. She's around to answer — but she'll only tell you what you actually ask.
body_json.subtitle: TKT-4471 · Checkout sometimes fails
blocks: hey! checkout's been flaky since the weekend — some people say they hit pay and nothing happens. a few orders def came through fine though. can you look before it costs us more sales? 🙏

body_json.prompt: Restate the ticket in your own words, then ask Maya whatever you need before you propose anything.

persona.name: Maya
persona.style: Non-technical solo founder. Lowercase, friendly. Answers ONLY what is literally asked. Never volunteers a hidden fact; a vague question gets a vague answer, a sharp targeted question reveals the matching fact.

first_turn_chips: Is it all shoppers or only some?
What does the shopper see when it fails?

scoring_rubric.distinguishing_observable: Did they surface the #1 fact (it started with Friday's deploy / the new discount column) unprompted, and order their questions by impact rather than volume?

rubric_exemplars.below: Says 'so checkout is broken, I'll look at the payment integration' and asks nothing, or only cosmetic questions Maya already answered.
rubric_exemplars.meets: Restates the flakiness accurately and asks at least one question on genuinely missing info, e.g. 'is it all shoppers or only some?'
rubric_exemplars.above: Restates AND probes the deploy first ('what changed Friday?'), then narrows the variant ('do the failing carts share anything — discount vs no discount?') — questions ordered by leverage.

gates.ai_floor: An AI summarises the ticket and suggests generic checkout debugging. It will not, unprompted, connect the timing to Friday's deploy or single out the no-discount cart variant — that connection is the signal.
gates.narrative_handoff: okay you actually get it. so — how do you want to fix this? i pulled up the code for you.

gates.title: Pick how you'll fix it

body_json.prompt: You know the shape now: carts created before Friday's deploy have a null discount and blow up checkout. There's more than one defensible fix. Maya wants the discount feature kept.
body_json.subtitle: Same ticket — decide the approach

options.label: Fix it at the database: give discount_pct a default of 0 and backfill the null rows.
Fix it in the app: guard the null in the adapter (?? 0) and correct the type.
Roll back Friday's deploy, then redo the discount feature properly.

body_json.prompt: Why this one under the constraints — and what would make you switch?

why_this.placeholder: Why this approach, given the discount feature must stay…

switch_if.placeholder: …the DB migration if we couldn't ship app code fast / …rollback if it spread

stronger_option.why: A default-and-backfill at the column fixes the class of bug for every consumer of the cart, not just this one read path — the null can never reach app code again. Defensible-strongest for a value that should always exist.

hidden_state.tradeoff_axis: defense-in-depth at the data layer vs speed/locality of an app-layer guard (and vs throwing away shipped work in a rollback)
hidden_state.switch_condition_expected: Would switch to the app-layer guard (B) if a prod migration is risky/slow to ship right now (do B to stop the bleeding, then backfill); rollback (C) only if the discount feature itself is broken beyond this null.
hidden_state.founder_pushback: wouldn't the quick code fix (option b) be faster though?

scoring_rubric.distinguishing_observable: Did they commit to a defensible option for the real reason (where the null should be prevented) AND name a concrete switch condition, holding under one pushback — rather than picking 'simplest' blindly?

stronger_option.why: Prevents the null at the layer every consumer reads, not just this path.

rubric_exemplars.below: Can't commit, or picks rollback/B only because it's 'easiest' with no mention of the null or the discount constraint.
rubric_exemplars.meets: Picks A or B with a valid reason tied to the null discount.
rubric_exemplars.above: Commits with the tradeoff named (prevent-at-source vs speed) AND a real switch condition, and holds when Maya pushes back on the simpler option.

gates.ai_floor: AI will list all three options with balanced pros/cons and not commit. The signal is the human picking under the stated constraint and naming when they'd switch.
gates.narrative_handoff: got it, noted. the codebase is open for you — go find where it's actually breaking before you change anything.

gates.title: Find what's actually broken

body_json.prompt: Here's the slice that runs checkout. Carts with no discount fail; discounted carts are fine, and the shopper just sees a spinner. Find the ONE block that's actually broken before you touch anything.
body_json.subtitle: Read-only · AI is off for this gate
blocks: Repro: processCheckout({ discount_pct: null, items:[…] }) → { ok:false, reason:'checkout_error' }

body_json.ai_off_note: You're navigating unaided — AI is off for this gate.
body_json.instruction: Mark the ONE block that's actually the cause (not just where it crashes).

distractor_blocks.why_tempting: This is where it THROWS (.toFixed on null), so it looks like the bug — but the value should never have been null here; the cause is upstream.
The catch block 'swallows' the error and returns the generic failure — looks like the cause of the silent fail, but it's only the symptom site.

four_tuple.flaw_location: adapter.ts: `discount_pct: row.discount_pct as number` — a cast that asserts non-null on a nullable column.
four_tuple.the_tell: Fails only for the no-discount (null) variant; the cast makes TS think it's always a number so nothing flags it.
four_tuple.catching_looks_like: Marks the adapter cast as the cause.
four_tuple.correcting_looks_like: Marks the cast AND explains: the null flows from the adapter into total.ts where .toFixed throws, caught in checkout.ts as a silent fail.

scoring_rubric.distinguishing_observable: Did they mark the adapter cast (the originating cross-file cause), not the throw site in total.ts or the catch in checkout.ts — via an efficient path?

four_tuple.flaw_location: adapter.ts lying `as number` cast on a nullable column.
four_tuple.the_tell: Only the null-discount variant fails; the cast suppresses the type error.
four_tuple.correcting_looks_like: Marks the cast and traces null → total.toFixed throw → checkout silent catch.

rubric_exemplars.below: Marks the .toFixed line in total.ts or the catch in checkout.ts (symptom sites).
rubric_exemplars.meets: Marks the `as number` cast in adapter.ts as the cause.
rubric_exemplars.above: Marks the cast and explains the cross-file mechanism, reaching it via a direct path from the symptom.

gates.ai_floor: Single-file AI review calls each file 'fine' — in isolation each is. The bug is only in the adapter→total→checkout relationship, which needs all three files and the right framing.
gates.narrative_handoff: yep, that's the one. okay — fix it for real now. here's the bar you're hitting.

gates.title: Ship the fix

body_json.prompt: For this session, make checkout succeed for a no-discount cart and keep every total correct — without breaking discounted carts. Edit the code and run the tests until they're green.
body_json.subtitle: The build · take your time
blocks: Done = all tests pass and a no-discount cart checks out with the right total.
Keep one consistent model — don't paper over the null in three places.
body_json.highlight: Use your normal AI tooling. This is the open-time gate — take your time.

tests_visible_names: discount cart still applies the discount
checkout succeeds for a no-discount cart
totals are always finite (no NaN, no throw)

body_json.prompt: Where did you verify the pieces actually agree (the adapter, the total, the row shape)?

hidden_state.canonical_assignment: Regardless of the G2 pick, in this sandbox the fix is the app-layer guard: make adaptCart coalesce the nullable discount_pct (e.g. `row.discount_pct ?? 0`) and correct the type so the null never reaches total.ts.

test_suite.name: discount cart still applies the discount
checkout succeeds for a no-discount cart
totals are always finite (no NaN, no throw)

hidden_state.pass_criteria: All four tests green AND the null is fixed at the adapter seam (not patched separately in total.ts and checkout.ts).

scoring_rubric.distinguishing_observable: Do the tests actually go green (the no-discount cart checks out with total 1000 and discounted carts still get 900), and is there ONE consistent fix at the adapter — not duplicated null-guards smeared across files?
scoring_rubric.working_output_definition: processCheckout(no-discount cart) === { ok:true, total_cents:1000 }; discounted cart still totals 900; no throw / no NaN.

rubric_exemplars.below: Tests stay red, or it only stops the throw without producing the correct total, or the build isn't completed.
rubric_exemplars.meets: All tests green via a working fix that meets the working-output definition.
rubric_exemplars.above: All tests green AND the null is handled once at the adapter seam with the type corrected — no duplicated guards, no lying cast left behind.

gates.ai_floor: AI will happily emit a null-guard the moment it's pointed at the throw — and will often patch total.ts or checkout.ts (the symptom sites) instead of the adapter. The signal is fixing the seam once, in the right place, and verifying it.
gates.narrative_handoff: tests are green — amazing, thank you. oh wait, hold on — one more thing before my investor call in two minutes…

gates.title: Two minutes to the investor call

body_json.prompt: maya: investors are on in 2 min and they LOVED the discount idea. can we just turn on a flat 20% off for everyone right now? like, today? 🙏🙏
body_json.subtitle: Decision, not a build — you don't have to finish anything.
body_json.reassure: We're watching how you decide, not whether you finish.

options.label: Yes-ish: put it behind a flag with a guardrail, after we confirm the null fix is live.
Not yet: we just fixed checkout — don't stack a risky global change before it's verified in prod.
Quick hack now, real version + tests right after the call.

strong_vs_weak.strong: Scopes the smallest safe slice and names the blast radius: confirm the checkout fix is live first, then a 20%-off can go behind a flag / limited rollout — explicitly ties the new risk to the bug just fixed.
strong_vs_weak.weak: Just says 'yes, on it!' and tries to ship a global 20% discount in two minutes, ignoring that checkout was broken minutes ago; or freezes and gives no decision.

hidden_state.tension_with_g2: Whatever careful fix they chose in G2, they're now pressured to bolt a broad, untested pricing change onto the same just-stabilised checkout path under a hard deadline.

scoring_rubric.distinguishing_observable: Under the clock, did they make a defensible judgment call (scope it / flag the risk / reasoned punt) instead of blindly complying or freezing?

rubric_exemplars.below: Blindly agrees and tries to build the global discount in 2 minutes, or gives no decision.
rubric_exemplars.meets: Makes a clear call (e.g. behind a flag, or 'not before it's verified') with a plausible one-line reason.
rubric_exemplars.above: Makes the call, names the blast radius / smallest safe slice / explicit risk flag, and ties it back to the checkout fix that just shipped.

gates.ai_floor: An AI asked to 'add a 20% discount' will just start writing the change. It won't weigh that checkout was broken 90 seconds ago or scope a safe rollout — that judgment is the whole signal.
gates.narrative_handoff: okay — good call, that's the fire out. let me tell you what i noticed while you worked…

skill_nodes_covered.node: cross-file debugging / root-cause navigation (distrust the green check)
build, integrate & verify the seam with AI tooling
judgement under pressure / scoping a safe change
